Precondition: User is on login page; Google client ID is loaded; user has a valid Google account
Steps: 1. Navigate to candidate.meetaiva.in | 2. Click Sign in with Google button | 3. Select Google account in OAuth popup | 4. Authorize the application
Expected Result: User is redirected to /dashboard; sessionData is stored in localStorage with jwt_token, email, candidateId; candidateInfo is stored in localStorage

Steps: 1. Click Sign in with Google | 2. Complete Google OAuth | 3. Backend returns 401 (expired/invalid token)
Expected Result: Snackbar shows 'Session expired or invalid. Redirecting to login.'; session data is cleared; user is redirected to / after 2 seconds

Steps: 1. Click Sign in with Google | 2. Complete Google OAuth
Expected Result: Snackbar displays 'We're currently overwhelmed with registrations. Please try again after some time.'

Steps: 1. Enter valid username in username field | 2. Enter valid password in password field | 3. Click Sign In button
Expected Result: Loading spinner appears on button; on success user redirected to /dashboard; sessionFlag is set to MOCK_INTERVIEW

Steps: 1. Leave username field empty | 2. Leave password field empty | 3. Click Sign In
Expected Result: Snackbar error: 'Please enter both username and password'; no API call is made

Precondition: User enters wrong username or password
Steps: 1. Enter invalid username | 2. Enter invalid password | 3. Click Sign In
Expected Result: Snackbar error: 'Invalid credentials. Please check your username and password.'

Steps: 1. Enter username | 2. Enter password | 3. Press Enter key
Expected Result: Login is triggered (same as clicking Sign In button)

Steps: 1. Enter text in password field | 2. Click the eye icon to toggle visibility | 3. Click eye icon again
Expected Result: Password toggles between masked (dots) and plain text; icon changes between eye and eye-off

Precondition: NEXT_PUBLIC_PROFILE is set to aws; user has Cognito credentials
Steps: 1. Enter email | 2. Enter password | 3. Click Sign In
Expected Result: Cognito tokens are obtained; JWT is decoded; email is extracted; backend auth succeeds; user redirected to /dashboard

Precondition: User is logged in with a JWT that is about to expire
Steps: 1. Wait for JWT to expire | 2. Observe behavior
Expected Result: setupTokenExpirationChecker detects expiry; logoutUser() clears sessionData, candidateInfo, user, candidateConsent from localStorage; user redirected to /

Precondition: User is on login page; statistics API is available
Steps: 1. Navigate to login page | 2. Observe the right section
Expected Result: Statistics cards display with animated count-up numbers; live pulsing green dot is visible; stats include candidates count, mentors, interviews, etc.

Steps: 1. Disconnect network | 2. Attempt any login method
Expected Result: Snackbar shows 'Network error. Please check your internet connection and try again.'

Test Case Name: Session Cleared on Login Page Load
Precondition: User has stale session data in localStorage
Steps: 1. Navigate to login page
Expected Result: sessionData, candidateInfo, user, candidateConsent are cleared from localStorage on component mount

Precondition: User is logged in with valid session
Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard
Expected Result: Dashboard loads within Layout; Sidebar visible on left; Navbar visible on top; summary stat cards displayed with data from fetchCandidateDashboardSummaryStats API

Precondition: User is logged in; has some activity history
Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard | 2. Observe summary cards section
Expected Result: Cards display: Total Activities count, Current Streak (days), Applied Jobs Count, Total Mocks, Total Resumes, Total Goals; each card has appropriate icon and label

Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard | 2. Observe engagement section
Expected Result: Activity calendar heatmap renders with color-coded daily activity levels; recent actions list shows latest activities with timestamps

Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard | 2. Observe resume stats card
Expected Result: Displays total resumes count and enhanced resumes count from fetchCandidateResumeStats

Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard | 2. Observe mock interview stats
Expected Result: Shows technicalPercent, communicationScore; skill breakdown with individual scores; data from fetchCandidateMockStats

Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard | 2. Observe job stats section
Expected Result: Displays top roles list, application trends chart, and recent applications list from fetchCandidateJobStats

Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard | 2. Observe coding assessment section
Expected Result: Shows overallScore and skill levels from fetchCandidateCodingAssessmentStats

Precondition: Newly registered user with no activity
Steps: 1. Log in as new user | 2. Navigate to /dashboard
Expected Result: Summary cards show zero values (0 activities, 0 streak, 0 jobs, 0 mocks); no errors; empty state messages or placeholders displayed where appropriate

Steps: 1. Open dashboard on desktop (>768px) | 2. Resize browser to mobile width (<768px) | 3. Click hamburger menu
Expected Result: Desktop: sidebar is visible and collapsible; Mobile: sidebar auto-collapses, hamburger menu appears in navbar, clicking it opens overlay drawer

Steps: 1. Observe sidebar navigation items
Expected Result: Sidebar shows: Dashboard (Home), Profile, Resumes, Jobs, Preps, AI Mentor; each with appropriate icon; current page is highlighted

Steps: 1. Click collapse/expand toggle on sidebar | 2. Observe sidebar state | 3. Refresh page
Expected Result: Sidebar toggles between collapsed (icons only) and expanded (icons + labels) view; state is persisted in localStorage (sidebarCollapsed) across page refreshes

Precondition: User is logged in with profile details
Steps: 1. Observe sidebar user section (when expanded)
Expected Result: Shows user avatar, name, role; contact details (phone, email, address) visible when expanded; 'Please upload resume' shown if no profile details

Test Case Name: Profile Page Load with Resume Data
Precondition: User is logged in; has uploaded at least one resume
Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard/profile
Expected Result: Profile header shows name, title/role, avatar; sections display: Basic Info (email, phone, location, about), Education, Skills (categorized), Languages, Work Experience, Projects, Awards/Certifications

Precondition: User is logged in; no resume uploaded
Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard/profile
Expected Result: Page shows 'resume not found' state or placeholder; Upload Resume button is prominently displayed

Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard/profile | 2. Scroll to GitHub section
Expected Result: Summary cards show Total Stars, Forks, Repos, Followers; Top Languages proportional bar chart; Contribution Activity calendar heatmap; Top Repositories cards with stars/forks

Precondition: User has no GitHub profile linked
Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard/profile | 2. Observe GitHub section
Expected Result: GitHub section shows appropriate empty state or is hidden; no error is thrown

Precondition: User has profile data from uploaded resume
Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard/profile | 2. Click Download Profile button
Expected Result: PDF is generated using jsPDF; two-column layout with sidebar (contact, skills, languages) and main content (summary, experience, projects, education, awards); file downloads to user's device

Steps: 1. Click Upload Resume button | 2. Select a PDF/DOCX file | 3. Confirm upload
Expected Result: File is uploaded via uploadResume() API; coins are deducted; profile data refreshes with newly parsed information; profile_details_updated event is dispatched

Precondition: User has resume with skills parsed
Steps: 1. Navigate to profile | 2. Observe Skills section
Expected Result: Skills are displayed categorized by skill categories with individual keywords; each category is visually distinct

Precondition: User has resume with education details
Steps: 1. Navigate to profile | 2. Observe Education section
Expected Result: Each education entry shows: institution name, degree, field, start date, end date; multiple entries shown if applicable

Precondition: User has resume with work experience
Steps: 1. Navigate to profile | 2. Observe Work Experience section
Expected Result: Each entry shows: company name, position/title, dates, description; entries sorted chronologically

Test Case Name: Profile - Real-time Update on Resume Upload
Steps: 1. Upload a new resume (from profile or another page) | 2. Observe profile page
Expected Result: profile_details_updated custom event triggers re-fetch; profile data updates without manual page refresh

Precondition: User is logged in; has sufficient coins
Steps: 1. Navigate to Resumes page | 2. Click Upload Resume | 3. Select a PDF or DOCX file | 4. Confirm upload
Expected Result: File uploads successfully; resume is parsed; new resume appears in the list; coins are deducted; profile_details updated in localStorage; snackbar shows success message

Steps: 1. Attempt to upload a resume
Expected Result: checkCoinsAvailable() throws; insufficientCoins event dispatched; BuyCoinsModal is displayed

Steps: 1. Navigate to Resumes page
Expected Result: All resumes listed with: file name, upload date, default indicator; default resume is visually marked

Precondition: User has uploaded a resume and a job is selected
Steps: 1. Click Enhance / magic wand button on a job | 2. Wait for AI enhancement to complete | 3. Review side-by-side comparison in ResumeEnhancer modal | 4. Accept/Edit/Discard individual sections | 5. Click Save
Expected Result: ResumeEnhancer modal opens; shows original vs enhanced for each section (Experience, Education, Skills, Projects, Awards); each section has Accept/Edit/Discard buttons; Save calls updateEnhancedResume(); fitment score shown before and after

Precondition: User has enhanced resume open in modal
Steps: 1. Make edits to enhanced sections | 2. Click Re-Score button
Expected Result: screenEnhancedResume() is called; new fitment score is displayed; progress bar updates

Steps: 1. Open ResumeEnhancer modal | 2. Click Download button | 3. Select PDF from dropdown
Expected Result: PDF file is generated and downloaded via downloadEnhancedResume() API

Steps: 1. Open ResumeEnhancer modal | 2. Click Download button | 3. Select DOCX from dropdown
Expected Result: DOCX file is generated and downloaded

Steps: 1. Navigate to Resumes page | 2. Click Set as Default on a non-default resume
Expected Result: Selected resume is marked as default via setDefaultResume() API; previously default resume loses default status; visual indicator updates

Precondition: User has at least one resume
Steps: 1. Click delete icon on a resume | 2. Confirm deletion in modal
Expected Result: Resume is removed via deleteCandidateResume() API; resume disappears from list; success snackbar shown

Steps: 1. Open ResumeEnhancer modal | 2. Observe missing requirements section
Expected Result: Missing requirements block shows context and reason for each gap; copy-to-clipboard functionality works

Precondition: User is logged in; jobs exist in the system
Steps: 1. Navigate to /dashboard/jobs | 2. Observe Available tab (default)
Expected Result: Job cards display with: title, company name, location, salary range, employment type, posted date (relative), fitment score (color-coded), skills/requirements list

Steps: 1. Navigate to Jobs page | 2. Click Applied tab
Expected Result: Shows list of applied jobs with application status

Steps: 1. Type keyword in search bar (e.g. Frontend) | 2. Observe filtered results
Expected Result: Job list filters in real-time to show only matching jobs; search is case-insensitive

Precondition: Jobs with various fitment scores exist
Steps: 1. Navigate to Jobs page | 2. Observe fitment scores on job cards
Expected Result: High Potential (>=55%): green indicator; Good Match (>=50%): yellow/amber indicator; Low Match (<50%): red indicator; AI fitment feedback recommendation displayed

Precondition: User has sufficient coins and at least one resume
Steps: 1. Click Upload JD button | 2. Step 1: Select JD file (PDF/DOCX) | 3. Step 2: Select existing resume or upload new | 4. Confirm
Expected Result: JD is uploaded via createJd(); coins deducted; new job appears in list with fitment score; success notification shown

Steps: 1. Click Create JD button | 2. Enter role (e.g. Backend Developer) | 3. Enter skills (e.g. Java, Spring Boot) | 4. Enter experience level | 5. Submit
Expected Result: AI generates complete JD via generateJd(); coins deducted; new job appears in list

Precondition: Job exists with JD; user has sufficient coins
Steps: 1. Click Practice button on a job card | 2. Wait for mock interview creation
Expected Result: prepareForMockInterviewByJD() creates interview session; coins deducted; user navigated to interview page

Steps: 1. Click trash/delete icon on a job | 2. Confirm deletion in modal
Expected Result: deleteJdForCandidate() removes JD; job disappears from list; confirmation modal closes; success notification

Steps: 1. Click on a job card to open details
Expected Result: Modal shows full job description, complete skills list (expandable), salary range, location, employment type, posted date, number of applicants, AI fitment feedback with justification

Precondition: Job has many skills that exceed one line
Steps: 1. Observe job card with many skills | 2. Click See more | 3. Click See less
Expected Result: Initially shows truncated skills; See more expands to full list with animation; See less collapses back

Precondition: Job and resume exist; user has coins
Steps: 1. Click magic wand / Enhance button on job card | 2. Wait for enhancement
Expected Result: Loading animation with rotating messages (Polishing your achievements etc.); ResumeEnhancer modal opens with enhanced resume; coins deducted

Steps: 1. Open JD Search modal | 2. Enter search keywords | 3. Adjust time filter (7 days / 30 days / All) | 4. View ranked results
Expected Result: Jobs ranked by fitment score via fitmentScoreBatchForCandidate(); each result shows match score, title, company, location, key skills; Add button to add job to personal list; checkmark for already-added jobs

Steps: 1. Click Preps in sidebar | 2. Navigate to /dashboard/preps
Expected Result: Page loads with existing prep items (JDs); create/upload options visible; each prep item shows job details and action buttons

Precondition: User has coins; is on Preps page
Steps: 1. Click Create JD | 2. Fill in role, skills, experience | 3. Submit form
Expected Result: generateJd() creates JD; coins deducted; new prep item appears in list; success notification

Precondition: User has coins and a resume
Steps: 1. Click Upload JD | 2. Select JD file | 3. Select resume | 4. Confirm
Expected Result: createJd() processes file; fitment score calculated; prep item added; coins deducted

Steps: 1. Open Upload JD modal | 2. Observe resume selection dropdown
Expected Result: Dropdown lists all resumes from getAllResumesForCandidate(); option to upload new resume is available; selected resume is used for fitment matching

Precondition: Prep item exists with JD; user has coins
Steps: 1. Click Practice button on a prep item
Expected Result: prepareForMockInterviewByJD() creates session; loading spinner shown; user navigated to interview page; coins deducted

Steps: 1. Click delete icon on a prep item | 2. Confirm in deletion modal
Expected Result: deleteJdForCandidate() removes item; item disappears from list; success notification

Precondition: Prep item with JD and resume exist
Steps: 1. Click enhance/magic button on prep item
Expected Result: enhanceResume() called; loading animation shown; ResumeEnhancer modal opens with side-by-side comparison; coins deducted

Steps: 1. Click AI Mentor in sidebar | 2. Navigate to /dashboard/aimentor
Expected Result: Page shows career goals: role-based options (Backend Dev, Frontend Dev, Data Science, etc.) and skill-based options (Database, DevOps, etc.); each with icon and description

Steps: 1. Click on a role card (e.g. Backend Developer) | 2. Review confirmation modal with goal details | 3. Click Start
Expected Result: createMentorshipSession() called; coins deducted; Hume EVI voice interface (MentorshipControls) opens; connection established

Steps: 1. Observe MentorStartCall component | 2. Wait for connection
Expected Result: 60-second countdown timer displayed; connect button with phone icon; Connecting to mentor... status; on success timer stops and voice session begins

Precondition: Mentorship session created; Hume API unreachable
Steps: 1. Wait for 60-second countdown to expire
Expected Result: User redirected to error page; appropriate error message displayed

Test Case Name: Voice Session - Auto Reconnect on Network Drop
Steps: 1. Simulate network disconnection | 2. Observe reconnection behavior
Expected Result: Network banner shows disconnection status; auto-reconnect attempts (up to 3 with 2-second delays); reconnection attempts counter displayed (e.g. Attempt 1/3); on reconnect success, session resumes

Precondition: User has completed at least one mentorship session for a goal
Steps: 1. Navigate to AI Mentor page | 2. Observe roadmap section
Expected Result: Weekly breakdown of learning topics and milestones displayed; roadmap data from getRoadmapByMenteeAndMentorshipSessionId()

Precondition: Active mentorship session; user has coins
Steps: 1. Click upload code button | 2. Select ZIP file | 3. Submit
Expected Result: uploadCodeAssignmentZip() processes file; coins deducted; analysis status shown (pending > processing > complete); notification updates shown

Precondition: Active mentorship session; user has coins
Steps: 1. Enter GitHub repository URL | 2. Submit
Expected Result: uploadCodeAssignmentGithubUrl() processes URL; coins deducted; analysis results shown

Precondition: User has an incomplete mentorship session
Steps: 1. Navigate to AI Mentor page with session query parameter | 2. Observe session loading
Expected Result: fetchMentorshipSessionById() loads previous session; voice session resumes with resumedChatGroupId; previous context preserved

Precondition: User has completed mentorship sessions with recordings
Steps: 1. Click Past Sessions button | 2. Observe modal
Expected Result: Modal displays video recordings from fetchMentorshipSessionDeliverableVideos(); each video has timestamp and playback controls

Steps: 1. Complete the mentorship conversation | 2. Voice connection closes
Expected Result: createMentorSummaryV2() generates summary (fire-and-forget); validateRoleTrack() generates roadmap (fire-and-forget); MentorSessionComplete modal appears with Session completed; auto-redirect to AI Mentor page

Steps: 1. Select a goal | 2. Attempt to start session
Expected Result: checkCoinsAvailable() fails; insufficientCoins event dispatched; BuyCoinsModal displayed

Precondition: User is logged in; WebSocket connected
Steps: 1. Observe notification bell icon in navbar
Expected Result: Badge component shows notification count; count updates in real-time as new WebSocket messages arrive

Steps: 1. Click notification bell icon
Expected Result: Anchored panel opens on right side; header shows Agent Notifications; list shows notifications with: request ID, status, timestamp; PuffLoader for in-progress items; progress bars for processing status

Steps: 1. Trigger a background task (e.g. resume enhancement) | 2. Observe notification bell
Expected Result: WebSocket at ws://{host}/api/v1/ws/incomingRequests?candidateId={id} receives messages; badge count increments; new notification appears in panel with progress updates

Steps: 1. Open notification panel | 2. Observe in-progress notification
Expected Result: Shows PuffLoader spinner; progress bar visible (0-100%); status text updates (e.g. Analyzing fitment...); on completion updates to success/error type

Steps: 1. Log in | 2. Observe notifications
Expected Result: Consent notification appears in notification area; clicking navigates to consent page; after consent given, consentUpdated event clears the notification

Steps: 1. Trigger a notification | 2. Wait 3 seconds
Expected Result: Notification automatically disappears after autoClose duration; persistent notifications (autoClose: null) remain until manually cleared or updated

Steps: 1. Click notification bell
Expected Result: Panel shows 'All Agents have completed!' message; no error

Precondition: User is logged in; WebSocket was connected
Steps: 1. Simulate network drop | 2. Restore network
Expected Result: WebSocket reconnects automatically; notifications resume; no duplicate notifications

Precondition: User is logged in with coins > 0
Steps: 1. Observe navbar
Expected Result: Coin icon with balance number displayed (e.g. coin icon + 15); balance matches candidateInfo.coins or sessionData.coins

Steps: 1. Observe navbar
Expected Result: Buy Coins button is displayed (on sm+ screens); coin balance number is hidden or shows 0

Steps: 1. Attempt a coin-deducting action (e.g. upload resume)
Expected Result: checkCoinsAvailable() throws; insufficientCoins custom event dispatched; BuyCoinsModal appears with: Buy Coins title with coin icon, Out of Coins message, How to Purchase section with email support & contact admin icons, Got it button

Steps: 1. Click Got it button | 2. OR click X button in header
Expected Result: Modal closes; no navigation or side effects

Precondition: User has coins; performs coin-deducting action
Steps: 1. Upload a resume (or enhance, or create mock) | 2. Observe coin balance in navbar
Expected Result: refetchCandidateCoins() is called; candidateInfoUpdated event dispatched; navbar coin display updates to new balance without page refresh

Steps: Verify coins are deducted for each: 1. Upload resume | 2. Enhance resume | 3. Create mock interview | 4. Create JD / Upload JD | 5. Create mentorship session | 6. Upload code assessment
Expected Result: Each operation deducts coins; balance decreases; refetchCandidateCoins() updates display

Steps: 1. Dispatch candidateInfoUpdated event | 2. Dispatch sessionDataUpdated event
Expected Result: Navbar re-reads candidateInfo or sessionData from localStorage; coin display updates accordingly

Steps: 1. Observe navbar right section
Expected Result: Profile avatar displayed (40x40px); if Google profile picture available, shows it; otherwise shows default avatar; dropdown chevron icon visible next to avatar

Steps: 1. Click on profile avatar/chevron in navbar
Expected Result: Dropdown menu opens; shows: user avatar, username, email address, credits info with coin icon, Logout button (red text)

Steps: 1. Observe dropdown content
Expected Result: Username matches localStorage.user.name; email matches localStorage.sessionData.email; credits match current coin balance

Steps: 1. Click Logout button
Expected Result: sessionData, candidateInfo, user, candidateConsent, profile_details cleared from localStorage; user redirected to / (login page); WebSocket connection closed

Precondition: User logged in without Google profile picture or image fails to load
Steps: 1. Observe avatar in navbar
Expected Result: Default avatar/placeholder image shown (e.g. default-user.svg); no broken image icon

Steps: 1. Click anywhere outside the dropdown
Expected Result: Dropdown closes; no side effects

Steps: 1. Click hamburger menu | 2. Observe mobile sidebar drawer
Expected Result: Profile section shows avatar, name, contact details (phone, email, address); navigation items: Dashboard, Profile, Resumes, Jobs, Preps, AI Mentor; active item highlighted with gradient; Visit MeetAiva.in link at bottom

Steps: 1. Click a navigation item (e.g. Jobs)
